message:
fix(ci): provide redis-server for the `redis` feature's docket tests

After the dead-code cleanup in 288ece4, the Test×3 and Coverage jobs
surfaced a real failure that had been masked by the earlier cargo
metadata crash: 5 tests under `crates/fastmcp-server/src/docket.rs`
(test_redis_*) spawn a real `redis-server` subprocess to exercise the
`redis` feature's distributed docket backend, and the GH-hosted
runners don't ship redis-server.

  thread 'docket::tests::test_redis_cancel_pending_task' panicked at
    crates/fastmcp-server/src/docket.rs:1716:
    spawn redis-server: Os { code: 2, kind: NotFound, ... }
  test result: FAILED. 1457 passed; 5 failed; ...

Install it where the platform package manager makes it trivial:
- Linux: `sudo apt-get install -y redis-server`
- macOS: `brew install redis`
- Windows: skip those tests (`-- --skip test_redis_`); redis isn't
  shipped in stock GH Windows runner channels and the docket tests
  aren't Windows-specific anyway

Coverage (Linux) gets the same redis-server install since it
re-runs the same `--all-features` test set under llvm-cov.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -55,9 +55,26 @@ jobs:
         with:
           key: ${{ matrix.os }}
 
-      - name: Run tests
+      # The `redis` feature's tests spawn a real `redis-server` subprocess
+      # (see crates/fastmcp-server/src/docket.rs).  Install it where the
+      # package manager makes it trivial; on Windows we skip those tests
+      # below since redis-for-Windows isn't shipped on the GH runners.
+      - name: Install redis-server (Linux)
+        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
+        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y redis-server
+
+      - name: Install redis-server (macOS)
+        if: runner.os == 'macOS'
+        run: brew install redis
+
+      - name: Run tests (Linux/macOS)
+        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
         run: cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --verbose
 
+      - name: Run tests (Windows, skip redis)
+        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
+        run: cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --verbose -- --skip test_redis_
+
       - name: Run doc tests
         run: cargo test --workspace --doc --all-features
 
@@ -103,6 +120,11 @@ jobs:
         with:
           tool: cargo-llvm-cov
 
+      # Coverage runs the same redis-feature tests as the Test job above,
+      # so it needs the same redis-server binary available on PATH.
+      - name: Install redis-server
+        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y redis-server
+
       - name: Run coverage
         run: |
           cargo llvm-cov --all-features --workspace \
